## LETS DISCOVER MORE

CareLabs provides businesses in Taiwan with a specialized Motor start research and analysis service. The primary purpose of our motor start analysis service is to evaluate the non-typical impacts of starting a large motor. In addition to appropriately connecting the electric motor to the line voltage, there are several other criteria to assess before starting an electric motor, such nuisance tripping, excessive nominal currents, and flickering or fading light, all of which indicate a faulty power supply. CareLabs will assign qualified personnel to your location to collect current and historical motor installation data. Then, using the acquired data and manufacturer input, calculations are performed to forecast future consequences, and recommendations for mitigating the impacts are provided.

**Both static and dynamic simulation approaches may be utilized to evaluate motor starting procedures:**

- The measured starting impedance of the electric motor is modelled to get the solution for the load flow.
- The short-circuit technique is used in pre-fault short circuit circumstances, whereas voltage drop calculations are used for motor starting currents.
- The dynamic model of the electric motor may be used to determine the effect if a detailed dynamic model of the electric motor’s circuit and load-torque characteristics is available. of motor starting in a conventional transient stability procedure.
- When extensive dynamic model information for an electric motor is not available, transient stability simulations can be employed.

## Motor Start Analysis Is Required in Taiwanese:

The use of motors in contemporary industrial systems and the size of electric motors are increasing dramatically. When a large motor linked to a line is started, it can disrupt the electric motor, locally connected loads, and electrically distant buses. The terminal voltage should be at or above 80% of the rated voltage when starting an electric motor. The square of a motor’s terminal voltage is exactly proportional to torque; hence voltage difference affects the load torque of a motor. In the event of a substantial voltage drop, running machines may approach their breaking torque and decelerate or halt. Voltage drops influence, among other things, electrical equipment, sensitive control devices, and lighting loads. In addition, a large initial current reduces terminal voltage, which increases the current taken by all systems supplying a continuous load. Additionally, defective low-voltage relays are identified.

## When Is a Motor Start Analysis Necessary?

- Prior to or during the acquisition of a large motor.
- If no generator is provided and the motor rating exceeds 30% of the base KVA rating of the supply transformer.
- If the generator is the sole source of power for the system and the motor rating exceeds 10-15% of the generator’s KVA rating.
- If the generator is the only source of power for the system. If many motors start concurrently in an industrial setting, a motor start study may be essential.
- Concerns with voltage loss while starting a motor with a large capacity are exacerbated by the limited capacity of smaller power supply. The examination of motor start-up aids comprehension of the output of systems incorporating minute generators.

## Motor Start Benefits Analysis:

Examination of motor launch Utilizing a motor or starting technique with favorable speed-torque characteristics is useful. To avoid exceeding the torque constraints of the equipment, any unexpected loads must be accelerated under supervision. At any time, the shaft functionality of the motor may be evaluated by completing a motor start study. Consequently, it aids in estimating the allowable torque constraints of the device. 

Studies of motors are starting to identify the machine’s voltage and whether an electric motor can start and accelerate. Skilled personnel at CareLabs conduct motor start studies to thoroughly evaluate your electric motor. We provide motor start analysis services in every major city in Taiwanese, including Taipei, Kaohsiung, Taichung, and Tainan. Reserve your motor starting study now or contact us for a price!
